Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Krishnas supremacy

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-11-25 · 6 answers
Hare Krishna Prji,
Please accept my humble obesciences,
All glories to sri guru and sri gauranga,
All glories to VEDA Pr,

I am in search of evidences "only" from Epic Mahabharata in which Bhagavan Krishna himself declares his supremacy (except in the BHAGAVAD-GITA)
i.e. before Kurukshetra war or after Kurukshetra war.
For instance,
I found one such dialog at
http://www.sacred-texts.com/hin/m14/m14054.htm (evidence which is post Kurukshetra war)

Please help me for this with the use of your vast knowledge and research in the Vedic literature.
user [38] · 2009-11-26
Hare Krishna Onkarji, same to you. Sorry, Im not acquainted with the whole Mbh although I have the full text on my hd and to search for keywords in such a huge work is very time consuming.
user [149] · 2009-11-26
Udyoga Parva in the Bhagavat-yana Parva, Chapter 131. Here Krishna displays His universal form to Duryodhana.
user [449] · 2009-11-26
Haribol no problems VEDA Prji .
even if it takes one month or more it is all right.

I will be very much obliged if out of your busy schedule you will try to search them.

Because these proofs are like hard rocks to smash the philosophies
which say that " knowledge of Gita was not spoken by Krishna but someone else. "
and which tilt the meaning of BG completely to come up with some ridiculous theories.
thank you very much...

haribol..
user [449] · 2009-11-26
thank you deena prji
user [38] · 2009-11-26
Onkarji, I dont have enough time for this, nor is Mbh considered the topmost pramana in GV. See quotes from Madhvacaryas Mahabharata tatparya nirnaya: http://connect.krishna.com/node/3957

Again, such ideas are not philosophies since theyre not based on pramanas. Not that someone comes up with some claim and then challenges others to refute it.
user [154] · 2009-11-26
>Mbh considered the topmost pramana in GV...

Hardly anywhere it is considered a good pramana. Too many versions.
